div1.entryFree:
Fleece, subst. 1) the wool shorn from a sheep at once: As II, 4, 79. H6C II, 5, 37. V, 6, 8. Figuratively: I would you had won the f. that he hath lost, Merch. III, 2, 245.
2) the skin of a sheep covered with wool: with her own white f. her voice controlled, Lucr. 678. the golden f. Merch. I, 1, 170. III, 2, 244. the Golden F. the highest order of knighthood in Burgundy: H6A IV, 7, 69.
3) curled hair: ere beauty's dead f. made another gay, Sonn. 68, 8. my f of woolly hair, Tit. II, 3, 34.
